name: H100 Architecture Optimization
approach: Target H100 architecture: 132 SMs, 192KB shared memory per SM, 3350 GB/s HBM3 bandwidth, 989 TFLOPS FP16. Use 256-thread blocks for element-wise, vectorized __nv_bfloat162 loads for 2x bandwidth. Grid sizing should be multiples of 132 for full SM utilization. Leverage 50MB L2 cache for reuse. Warp-level reductions with __shfl_xor_sync for 32-element synchronization.

evidence.strategy: Switch to warp-level shuffle reduction and shared memory tiling.
Switch to warp-level shuffle reduction using shared memory tiling to alleviate memory bottleneck.
Apply shared-memory histogram with privatized bins and warp-aggregated atomics to mitigate contention.
tune block sizes and optimize vectorized load patterns with grid-stride loops.

pitfalls: Block count must be multiple of 132 SMs for full utilization — odd grid sizes waste SMs
Shared memory per SM is 192KB but per-block limit depends on occupancy target
BF16 vectorized loads require 2-byte alignment — ensure tensor strides are even
